title: botrychium pumicola
intro: Botrychium pumicola, also known as pumice grape fern, is a species of rare and endangered plant in the family Ophioglossaceae. It is a type of vascular fern that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as arthritis, gout, and skin conditions, due to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Botrychium pumicola include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
